summary: Backend-only regression for Tag n Ride. Exercised auth, wallet, transfer, withdraw, transactions, driver lookup, and ratings via the public preview URL (https://ride-tagging.preview.emergentagent.com/api). 34/34 pytest cases pass including all role-based access checks and edge cases (insufficient balance, self-pay, duplicate ratings, unknown driver/transaction, missing/invalid JWT, duplicate phone, non-digit PIN).

minor.issue: amount fields in TopUp/Transfer/Withdraw use le=1_000_000 — over-cap requests return 422 (Pydantic) before reaching balance/role logic. Acceptable, but document for clients so 'insufficient balance' tests stay below 1,000,000 NGN.

When passenger has insufficient balance, server returns 400 ‘Insufficient balance’ even if the driver_user_id is invalid (lookup happens first → 404, but only if wallet check is skipped). Behaviour matches code (driver lookup runs before debit), just noting precedence.
Each transaction triggers a separate users.find_one for counterparty enrichment (N+1). Fine for now but consider $lookup or batched fetch for scale.

Rating allowed for any past payment txn regardless of txn.receiver_id matching driver_user_id — passenger could (in theory) rate any driver against any of their txns. Consider asserting txn.receiver_id == driver_user_id and txn.type == 'payment'.

action_items: (Optional, low priority) In /api/wallet/rate, also validate that txn.receiver_id == body.driver_user_id and txn.type == 'payment' to harden the rating system.
(Optional) Reduce N+1 user lookups in /api/wallet/transactions by batching counterparty fetches.

(Optional) Expo dependency version warnings in expo logs (async-storage, expo-camera, expo-haptics, expo-linear-gradient, react-native-svg) — not blocking backend tests but main agent may want to align with `expo install` recommendations.
critical_code_review_comments: JWT secret read from env at import time — good. Uses HS256 with 7-day TTL as documented.
bcrypt PIN hashing via bcrypt.hashpw + gensalt — correct ($2b$ format).

Atomic debit pattern uses find_one_and_update with {'balance': {'$gte': amount}} guard — prevents oversold race; transfer credits driver in a separate update (no Mongo multi-doc transaction). For production an explicit MongoDB session/transaction would be safer if the credit step fails after debit.
All endpoints exclude _id via projection {'_id': 0} — clean JSON.
CORS allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False — fine for Bearer-token mobile app (no cookies).
Duplicate-phone returns 400 (could be 409 Conflict, but 400 is acceptable and matches spec).
seed_data_creation: Each test run self-registers fresh TEST_Passenger / TEST_Driver users with timestamp-suffixed phones. No cleanup performed; created docs are prefixed with TEST_ in full_name for easy identification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (frontend/.env) — note the preview URL var is EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL, not EXPO_BACKEND_URL. JWT in Authorization: Bearer header. All amount fields capped at 1,000,000 by Pydantic (le=1_000_000) so 'too large' values return 422, not 400. Rating tests depend on a previous payment txn — use module-scoped fixtures and store the txn id (we used pytest._tagnride_payment_txn_id). Frontend tests not yet run — Expo web preview is up but main agent only requested backend.
rca_of_issue: No backend bugs found. The single test failure observed during the first run was a test-side bug: I sent amount=99,999,999 to /api/wallet/withdraw expecting a 400 'Insufficient balance', but Pydantic field cap (le=1_000_000) intercepts at validation layer and returns 422. Lowered to 999,999 (under cap, over balance) and the test now correctly verifies the 400 path.
